decision_target: Derive the final Lite execution API contract for ctx.exec dependency operations, explicit params, and the no-context-argument wall.
read_method: Read public types, runtime dispatch, extension boundaries, tests, lint rules, docs, git history for the explicit callback-parameter work, and repository ctx.exec/scope.run function call candidates at the exact head.

current_api_matrix.form: { name, deps, fn, params, tags?, signal? }
current_api_matrix.callback_context: fn receives inferred deps and params; no ctx or scope
Receives child ExecutionContext for observation and policy
current_api_matrix.status: keep; this is an extension boundary, not operation input

current_api_matrix.callback_context: Receives the context that defines the primitive lifecycle

recommended_target_contract.signature: type ExecDepsOptions<D extends Record<string, ExecutionDependency>, Args extends unknown[], Result> = { name: string; deps: D; params: Args; fn: (deps: InferDeps<D>, ...args: Args) => Result; tags?: Tagged<any>[]; signal?: AbortSignal; flow?: never } & NonStreaming<Result>
recommended_target_contract.scope_signature: run<const D extends Record<string, ExecutionDependency>, const Args extends unknown[], Result>(options: ExecDepsOptions<D, Args, Result>): Promise<Awaited<Result>>
recommended_target_contract.context_signature: exec<const D extends Record<string, ExecutionDependency>, const Args extends unknown[], Result>(options: ExecDepsOptions<D, Args, Result>): Promise<Awaited<Result>>
recommended_target_contract.zero_dependency_example: ctx.exec({ name: "client.send", deps: {}, params: [client, message], fn: (_deps, client, message) => client.send(message) })
recommended_target_contract.tag_example: ctx.exec({ name: "tenant.read", deps: { tenant: tags.required(tenant) }, params: [], tags: [tenant("acme")], fn: ({ tenant }) => tenant })

decisions.name: required because the one-off callback has no reusable graph handle supplying stable identity
decisions.deps: required; use deps: {} for zero graph dependencies
decisions.params: required; use params: [] for zero runtime inputs
decisions.callback: receives deps then params; never ctx or scope
decisions.tags: remain invocation-local; values needed by the callback are read through declared tag executors in deps
decisions.signal: remains an invocation lifecycle option; it combines with parent cancellation internally and is not injected into fn
decisions.streaming: rejected at type and runtime; use a generator flow with execStream/runStream
decisions.scope_lifecycle: scope.run owns and closes the temporary boundary
decisions.ctx_lifecycle: ctx.exec creates and closes a child inside the caller-owned managed boundary

allowed: Store params in childCtx.input for extensions.
Resolve declared deps against the child context.
Seed tags and combine signals on the child context.
Pass child context to wrapExec extensions.
Use context inside atom, flow, resource, and preset factories.
forbidden: Pass childCtx or scope into the public inline fn.
Allow deps, params, or name to be omitted.
Keep a second no-deps context-taking callback overload.
Let an inline callback close over graph or runtime inputs.

smallest_implementation_slice: Replace RunDepsOptions, RunFnOptions, and ExecFnOptions with one documented ExecDepsOptions shared by Scope.run and ExecutionContext.exec.
Add the deps overload to ExecutionContext.exec and remove the two context-taking inline overloads.
Make the internal inline executor resolve options.deps, then call options.fn(resolvedDeps, ...options.params). It may use childCtx internally but must not pass it to fn.
Delegate Scope.run deps execution through the same internal path while retaining scope-owned tags, signal, close result, and resource boundary.
Retain Extension.wrapExec(next, target, ctx). Update ExecTargetFn to the type-erased deps callback shape if callback targets remain observable.
Update lint so both scope.run and ctx.exec require declared deps and explicit params, reject captured inputs, and reject passing ctx or scope through params.
breaking_impacts: Remove ctx.exec({ fn: (ctx, ...params) => ... }) and scope.run({ fn: (ctx, ...params) => ... }).
Require name on every inline operation.
Existing callbacks must remove their first ctx parameter.
Code reading child tags, data, name, signal, resolve, or scope from the inline callback must reify that need as a declared dependency or a flow.
Extension code keeps ctx access; operation code does not.
The broad source scan found ctx.exec function candidates in 11 TypeScript files. An AST migration checker must produce the exact call count before acceptance.
Current foreign-call docs and examples contain stale captured or _ctx callback shapes.
required_tests: Type inference parity for scope.run and ctx.exec with atom, resource, tag, flow-controller, params, and awaited results.
Compile failures for missing name, deps, or params and for ctx-taking callbacks.
deps: {} plus params: [] as the sole zero-dependency form.
Dependency activation completes before fn runs and activation failure never calls fn.
Tags resolve through declared tag deps with invocation precedence.
Signal abort closes child resources and prevents post-close work without exposing ctx to fn.
Extensions observe ctx.name, ctx.input === params, tags, success, and failure.
Inline async iterables, iterators, and generators fail at type and runtime.
Scope-owned and managed-context resource cleanup remain distinct.
Lint rejects captures and direct ctx/scope params for both receivers while ignoring unrelated run/exec methods.
required_docs: pkg/core/lite/README.md API table and foreign integration section

requirements: Read the exact expected git head.
Use the TypeScript checker to confirm both overloads infer the same deps and params.
Fail on public inline fn signatures whose first parameter is ExecutionContext or Scope.
AST-scan ctx.exec and scope.run inline options for required name, deps, params, captures, and direct ctx/scope arguments.

questions_answered: ctx.exec should receive the same inferred deps callback form as scope.run.
name, deps, and params must be required.
deps: {} is the one zero-dependency form.
tags and signal remain invocation options and are not callback arguments.
Core factories and extensions retain context access; inline operation callbacks do not.
The legacy context-taking fn forms are redundant and conflict with the standing wall.
questions_unanswered: An independent validator has not replayed the proposed type surface.
The exact migration call count needs an AST checker.
Extension target identity behavior needs a regression decision: callback target versus synthetic flow target.
No product implementation or deterministic contract checker exists yet.

candidate_pkrs.assignment: Implement the shared ExecDepsOptions type and one internal execution path.
Migrate repository call sites, lint rules, and type/runtime tests.
Update public TSDoc, README, PATTERNS, migration, changelog, changeset, and examples.
Add and replay the exact-head inline execution contract checker.

active_anti_goal_verification.evidence_ref: No model-provider edit was made by this worker.

result.reason: The API decision is source-backed, but the exact-head checker and independent validation do not yet exist.

decision: Recommend one shared, required-name/deps/params inline execution form with no ctx or scope callback argument. Preserve context only inside core primitive factories and extension observation boundaries.
required_role_sentence: DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
